memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Origins Available: English, German, Italian, Spanish. Spelling variations of this family name include: Palmer, Pallmer, Parmer and others. First found in Yorkshire where they were seated from early times and their first records appeared on the early census rolls taken by the early Kings of Britain to determine the rate of taxation of their subjects. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: William Palmer, who arrived in Plymouth in 1621 aboard the " Fortune;" Frances Palmer, who arrived in Plymouth in 1623 aboard the "Anne and the Little James. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009), Motto Translated: The palm is for virtue.This ancient Anglo-French surname recorded in the spellings of Palmer, Palmar, Parmer, Paumier, and the dialectals Pymar, Pymer, and Pimer, is a medieval descriptive nickname. It is one of the sizable group of early European surnames that were gradually created from the habitual use of personal descriptions or characteristics. The derivation, in this instance, is from the pre 10th century Old French, "palmer or paumier", and derives from assumed or perhaps in some cases, actual, pilgrimages or crusades to the Holy Land. Such pilgrims, who often wore suits of armor and carried some very unfriendly weapons, generally brought back a palm branch as proof that they had actually made the journey. A quotation from a medieval writer reads, "The faded palm-branch in his hand, showed the pilgrim from the Holy Land". In its various spellings this was one of the earliest of all surnames. Early examples of the surname recordings include such as Wiger le Palmer of Lincolnshire in the 1191 rolls, and Richard le Paumere of Middlesex in the year 1198. Ricardus Palmer appears in the 1379 Poll Tax rolls of Yorkshire, whilst Joseph Pymer was recorded in London in 1665, at the height of the 'Black death'. John Palmer, aged 18 years, who embarked from London on the ship "Primrose" bound for Virginia, in July 1635, was one of the earliest recorded settlers in America. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling. Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

Origin Displayed: Italian - Origins Available: English, German, Italian, Spanish. Spelling variations of this family name include: Palma, Palmer, Palmeri, Palmaro, Palmerini, Palerino and many more. First found in Naples, formerly Napoli or Neapolis, formerly capital of the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies in southern Italy. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Matheo Palma, who arrived in New York city in 1882; Vito Palma, who arrived in New York on the S.S. Italia from Naples on 2 March 1913; Maria A DiPalma, who arrived in New York on the S.S. Vincenzo Florio from Palermo in 1881. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: The palm is for virtue. Origin Displayed: Spanish - Origins Available: English, German, Italian, Spanish. Spelling variations of this family name include: Palma, Palmas, Palmar, Lapalma, Palmer, De la Palma and many more. First found in Castile, in north central Spain. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Early migrants to the New World bearing this surname mostly settled in the Caribbean and in Central America. Thy included Antonio De Palma who arrived in Espanola in 1512. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). zh.Angmering Manor, Sussex, England, United: 英國英格蘭素可安莫林
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Palmer於1339年出生在英國素可斯易寧時，他的父親Adam Palmer21歲，母親Lady Mary Isabella Seddinghouse, Baroness Palmer22歲。他於1362年與Isabel Elizabeth Stopham在英國素可安莫林結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1380年在英國英格蘭素可安莫林享年41歲。

nameMeanings.name: Palmer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English palmer(e) ‘palmer, pilgrim to the Holy Land’ (Anglo-Norman French palmer, Old French pa(l)mer, paum(i)er), so called from the palm branch carried by such pilgrims. The term was also used to denote an itinerant monk who traveled from shrine to shrine under a vow of poverty. This surname is also common in Ireland, where it has been recorded from the 13th century onward.
Irish: when not of English origin (see 1 above), a surname adopted for Gaelic Ó Maolfhoghmhair (see Milford ), the name of an ecclesiastical family.
Swedish (mainly Palmér): ornamental name formed with palm ‘palm tree’ + the suffix -ér (a derivative of Latin -erius) or -er (from German).

variants.name: Milford
meanings: English (mainly Devon): habitational name from any of the many places called Milford, deriving from Old English myln ‘mill’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish (Antrim and Galway): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolfhoghmhair ‘descendant of Maolgfhoghmhair’, a personal name meaning ‘chief of harvest’. The Irish name was first Anglicized as Mullover, which was later assimilated to Milford. See also Palmer .

variants.name: Palmiere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Dante, Enrico, Nicola, Salvatore, Vittorio.

variants.name: Palmore
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Palmer   or an Americanized form of South German   Balmer  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Palmer was born in 1339, in Steyning, Sussex, England, his father, Adam Palmer, was 21 and his mother, Lady Mary Isabella Seddinghouse, Baroness Palmer, was 22. He married Isabel Elizabeth Stopham in 1362, in Angmering, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1380, in Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Palmer est né en 1339, à Steyning, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Adam Palmer, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Lady Mary Isabella Seddinghouse, Baroness Palmer, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Elizabeth Stopham en 1362, à Angmering, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1380, à Angmering, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Palmer 1339 in Steyning, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam Palmer 21 und seine Mutter Lady Mary Isabella Seddinghouse, Baroness Palmer 22. Er hat 1362 in Angmering, Sussex, England Isabel Elizabeth Stopham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1380 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Palmer è nato il nel 1339 nel Steyning, Sussex, England, il suo padre, Adam Palmer, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Lady Mary Isabella Seddinghouse, Baroness Palmer, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Isabel Elizabeth Stopham nel 1362, nel Angmering, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1380, nel Angmering, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 41 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Palmer(이)가 1339년 영국 수세스 스테이닝에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam Palmer(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Mary Isabella Seddinghouse, Baroness Palmer(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1362년 영국 수세스 앤그머잉에서 Isabel Elizabeth Stopham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 앤그머잉에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Palmer родился в 1339 г. в местности стининг, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Adam Palmer было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Mary Isabella Seddinghouse, Baroness Palmer, 22.
